questions.question: Why is Java considered platform-independent at the application level?
options: A) Because source code runs directly on hardware without any runtime
B) Because compiled bytecode can run on different systems where a compatible JVM exists
C) Because Java does not need compilation
D) Because operating systems ignore Java files
questions.correctAnswer: B) Because compiled bytecode can run on different systems where a compatible JVM exists

questions.question: What is the purpose of a class in Java?
options: A) It defines structure and behavior for objects
B) It is only used for database queries
C) It replaces methods entirely
D) It is a deployment artifact
questions.correctAnswer: A) It defines structure and behavior for objects

questions.question: Why is encapsulation useful?
options: A) It hides internal implementation and helps control how state is accessed or changed
B) It removes the need for methods
C) It automatically optimizes memory
D) It only matters in frontend design
questions.correctAnswer: A) It hides internal implementation and helps control how state is accessed or changed

questions.question: What is the practical role of interfaces in Java design?
options: A) Defining contracts that multiple classes can implement with different behavior
B) Replacing all classes
C) Storing SQL tables
D) Managing JVM memory directly
questions.correctAnswer: A) Defining contracts that multiple classes can implement with different behavior

questions.question: Why are generics useful in Java collections?
options: A) They provide stronger type safety and reduce casting mistakes
B) They convert Java into JavaScript
C) They replace iteration
D) They are only for UI applications
questions.correctAnswer: A) They provide stronger type safety and reduce casting mistakes

questions.question: What is the purpose of checked and unchecked exceptions?
options: A) They indicate different categories of failure handling expectations in code
B) They only exist to slow programs down
C) They replace validation logic
D) They are only used by the JVM internals
questions.correctAnswer: A) They indicate different categories of failure handling expectations in code

questions.question: Why does Java use garbage collection?
options: A) To automatically reclaim memory that is no longer reachable by the program
B) To compile SQL queries
C) To style output in the terminal
D) To replace all performance work
questions.correctAnswer: A) To automatically reclaim memory that is no longer reachable by the program

questions.question: What problem do streams solve in Java code?
options: A) They provide a more expressive way to transform and aggregate data collections
B) They replace databases
C) They are only for network sockets
D) They are required to use loops
questions.correctAnswer: A) They provide a more expressive way to transform and aggregate data collections

questions.question: Why is concurrency difficult in Java and other languages?
options: A) Because shared state and execution timing can create race conditions and subtle bugs
B) Because threads cannot run in Java
C) Because compilers forbid parallel tasks
D) Because APIs cannot be concurrent
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because shared state and execution timing can create race conditions and subtle bugs

questions.question: What is dependency injection meant to improve in backend applications?
options: A) Coupling, testability, and wiring of application components
B) Browser rendering speed
C) SQL indexing automatically
D) Terminal color themes
questions.correctAnswer: A) Coupling, testability, and wiring of application components

questions.question: Why are service and controller layers often separated in a Spring application?
options: A) To keep request handling and business logic responsibilities clearer and easier to maintain
B) To make deployment impossible
C) Because Java disallows large classes
D) To avoid using databases
questions.correctAnswer: A) To keep request handling and business logic responsibilities clearer and easier to maintain

questions.question: What does JPA primarily help with?
options: A) Mapping Java objects to relational persistence patterns
B) Styling API responses
C) Replacing SQL knowledge entirely
D) Managing browser cookies
questions.correctAnswer: A) Mapping Java objects to relational persistence patterns

questions.question: Why do transactions matter in database-backed applications?
options: A) They help preserve consistency for groups of related data changes
B) They only improve frontend performance
C) They are only for NoSQL systems
D) They replace validation
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help preserve consistency for groups of related data changes

questions.question: What is the value of unit tests in Java codebases?
options: A) They help verify small pieces of logic and reduce regressions during change
B) They eliminate the need for integration tests entirely
C) They automatically deploy services
D) They only test SQL performance
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help verify small pieces of logic and reduce regressions during change

questions.question: Why is authentication different from authorization in a backend system?
options: A) Authentication identifies who the user is, authorization decides what actions are allowed
B) Authorization happens before login, authentication after logout
C) They are the same concept
D) Only frontend apps need authentication
questions.correctAnswer: A) Authentication identifies who the user is, authorization decides what actions are allowed

questions.question: What is the operational value of structured logging in a Java service?
options: A) It makes debugging and monitoring production behavior more consistent
B) It replaces exceptions completely
C) It only matters for local scripts
D) It stores binary class files
questions.correctAnswer: A) It makes debugging and monitoring production behavior more consistent

questions.question: Why are build tools such as Maven or Gradle important?
options: A) They manage dependencies, build steps, and repeatable project workflows
B) They replace the JVM
C) They only help with CSS compilation
D) They are only for enterprise licenses
questions.correctAnswer: A) They manage dependencies, build steps, and repeatable project workflows

questions.question: What is one reason performance tuning in Java requires more than reading code alone?
options: A) Runtime behavior, allocations, IO, query patterns, and contention must be observed in context
B) Because Java cannot be profiled
C) Because the JVM always makes code optimal automatically
D) Because SQL has no effect on Java services
questions.correctAnswer: A) Runtime behavior, allocations, IO, query patterns, and contention must be observed in context

questions.question: Why is configuration externalization useful in backend systems?
options: A) It allows different environments to run the same application with different settings safely
B) It removes the need for deployment
C) It prevents all runtime errors
D) It is only useful for desktop apps
questions.correctAnswer: A) It allows different environments to run the same application with different settings safely

questions.question: What is the strongest learning approach for Java beyond syntax?
options: A) Memorize keywords only
B) Build real applications that combine language fundamentals, architecture, persistence, testing, and debugging
C) Ignore object-oriented design
D) Skip backend concerns completely
questions.correctAnswer: B) Build real applications that combine language fundamentals, architecture, persistence, testing, and debugging

questions.question: Which language is used for Android development?
options: A) Python
B) Java
C) C#
D) Swift
questions.correctAnswer: B) Java

questions.question: Which keyword is used for inheritance in Java?
options: A) implements
B) extends
C) inherits
D) super
questions.correctAnswer: B) extends
